Hero.subtitle: BI & Data Consultant with a Data Engineering core — building reliable pipelines, migrations, and dashboards. Currently exploring applied AI: LLMs, RAG, and AI agents.

Projects.homeIntro: Case studies from BI, data engineering, and applied-AI work — the outcomes I’m proud to have shipped.
Projects.listIntro: Anonymized case studies from BI, data engineering, and applied-AI projects.

CaseStudy.diagramCaption: Simplified data flow — illustrative, not a client system.

etl-modernization.title: Legacy ETL to Modern Orchestration
etl-modernization.summary: Migrating a legacy ETL orchestration to a modern cloud-native platform without disrupting nightly production pipelines.
etl-modernization.context: A large enterprise running SAP-integrated data operations needed to migrate legacy ETL orchestration to a modern cloud-native platform — without disrupting nightly production pipelines feeding inventory, logistics, and sales reporting.
approach: Ran a two-week parallel observation phase with the legacy and modern systems running side by side.
Built automated daily comparison scoring to catch discrepancies early.
Conducted a systematic configuration audit to catch environment-scoping risks before go-live.
etl-modernization.solution: Migrated 800+ pipeline tables across SAP source systems, CRM integrations, and a Snowflake target warehouse. Identified and documented 17 platform-level behavioral differences between the legacy and modern orchestration engines (data type handling, API batch limits, credential scoping, iteration performance).
etl-modernization.outcome: Reached a high automated match rate between legacy and modern pipeline outputs prior to cutover. The validation process also caught a critical data-integrity risk during testing — before it could affect production.

bi-architecture.title: Multi-Source BI Architecture
bi-architecture.summary: Consolidating scattered advertising data across many platforms into a unified, reusable reporting architecture.
bi-architecture.context: An agency managing advertising performance data across many clients and multiple ad platforms needed a unified reporting architecture instead of scattered, inconsistent exports.
approach: Designed a layered warehouse architecture (raw ingestion → staging → dimensional model) so each new platform could be onboarded with a reusable pattern rather than a bespoke pipeline.
Standardized incremental extraction and deduplication logic across all sources.
bi-architecture.solution: Consolidated multiple advertising-platform integrations into standardized dimensional models (campaigns, ad groups, accounts, performance) in Snowflake, and migrated the dashboard delivery layer to improve stakeholder self-service.
bi-architecture.outcome: New data sources can be onboarded using the same standardized pattern, cutting the setup work needed for each additional platform compared to building one-off pipelines.

reporting-automation.title: Automated Reporting Pipeline
reporting-automation.summary: An end-to-end automation connecting document upload, AI text extraction, and structured database entry.
reporting-automation.context: Manual budget tracking and reporting is time-consuming and error-prone for individuals and small businesses managing their own finances.
approach: Built an end-to-end automation pipeline connecting document upload, AI-powered text extraction, and structured database entry — removing manual data entry entirely.
reporting-automation.solution: Automation pipeline (Make) connecting cloud file storage → PDF/text extraction → AI-powered categorization via the Claude API → structured database (Notion), fully automated from upload to categorized entry.
reporting-automation.outcome: Personal/product project, still in active development — demonstrates end-to-end automation design beyond client BI work.

applied-ai-exploration.title: Applied AI Exploration
applied-ai-exploration.summary: A personal exploration of how LLMs can make BI tooling more accessible without compromising data security.
applied-ai-exploration.context: Personal exploration of how large language models can make BI tooling more accessible to non-technical users without compromising data security — a gap I see consistently in client work.
approach: Hands-on experimentation building with the Claude API — testing how LLMs can interpret, categorize, and summarize business data in ways non-technical stakeholders can use directly.
applied-ai-exploration.solution: Prototypes exploring LLM-assisted data categorization and natural-language interfaces over structured data.
applied-ai-exploration.outcome: Ongoing exploration — framed transparently as active learning, not a finished client deliverable.

executive-reporting-automation.title: Automated Executive Reporting
executive-reporting-automation.summary: Removing a critical single-person dependency from a weekly executive report — now fully automated with built-in quality checks.
executive-reporting-automation.context: A client's most business-critical weekly report — feeding senior leadership decisions every Monday morning — depended entirely on one person manually preparing and running a complex Python script at 6am each week. Any absence put a critical report at risk.
approach: Rather than patching the fragile script, rebuilt the pipeline from the ground up.
Designed a star-schema data model with SCD2 history tracking to preserve accurate point-in-time reporting.
Layered a dashboard on top, optimized for heavy, repetitive queries.
executive-reporting-automation.solution: Star-schema ETL with SCD2 history management, a dashboard layer with materialized views for query performance, and fully automated data-quality and consistency checks running before each report generation and delivery — no manual intervention required.
executive-reporting-automation.outcome: Eliminated the single-person dependency entirely. A report that once required someone to wake up at 6am to run it by hand now generates and delivers itself automatically every week, with built-in quality validation before it reaches decision-makers.

dashboard-poc.title: Dashboard Proof-of-Concept
dashboard-poc.summary: Rebuilding a dashboard from a Figma mockup against live warehouse data, in Scrum — where surfacing a real problem mattered more than a finished product.
dashboard-poc.context: A prospective client wanted to evaluate consulting capabilities through a proof-of-concept: rebuild a dashboard from their own Figma mockup, fully connected to live warehouse data, matching their intended design as closely as possible.
approach: Worked in Scrum with daily standups to track progress and adjust scope as the PoC evolved.
Built the real, data-connected version of the mockup rather than a static prototype.
dashboard-poc.solution: A dashboard built against live cloud data-warehouse sources, engineered to visually and functionally match the client's design specification.
dashboard-poc.outcome: The process surfaced a genuine internal data-quality issue on the client's side — significant enough that the PoC was paused as a result. Even so, the dashboard build itself drew strong positive feedback on execution quality — a case where the process working correctly (surfacing a real problem) mattered more than reaching a finished product.

About.name: Sylvana Ndemanou
About.bio1: A BI & Data Consultant with a Data Engineering core, based in Sherbrooke, QC. I build reliable data pipelines, lead ETL/ELT migrations, and design multi-source architectures that turn scattered data into dependable reporting.
About.bio2: At Codex Consulting I work across cloud data platforms — modernizing orchestration, consolidating sources, and automating reporting so teams can trust the numbers they act on.
About.bio3: I’m also actively exploring applied AI — LLMs, RAG, and AI agents — to make BI tooling more accessible to non-technical users without compromising data security.

Contact.heading: Let’s connect

Story.metaDescription: A more personal chapter — Sylvana Ndemanou's roots, journey, and what drives her.

cover.title: Where I come from
cover.subtitle: A more personal chapter — my roots, my journey, and what keeps me moving.

chapters.title: It started in Cameroon
body: I was born and raised in Cameroon — that warm, vibrant place is the root of who I am. It taught me to be curious, patient, and stubbornly hopeful.
I earned my baccalauréat in Mathematics and Life & Earth Sciences. Numbers and living systems fascinated me early — a quiet hint of the data path to come.

chapters.title: A leap to France
body: In late 2020, in the middle of the pandemic, I moved to Lyon to study Management at ECEMA. Everything was new at once: a culture, the codes of the professional world, friendships to rebuild from scratch.
It took time. I adapted, I stumbled, I kept going — and earned my bachelor's in Management. That season taught me how to feel like a stranger and turn it into strength.

chapters.title: Falling for the piano
body: With my very first paycheck, I bought myself a piano. I had played a little as a child before life made me stop — but I never forgot the feeling.
Music became a second language for me: when words fall short, I compose. (I also speak English, by the way — haha.)

chapters.title: North, to Canada
body: In the summer of 2023 I flew to Canada — with my piano, of course — to rejoin my family. That winter I began my master's in Business Intelligence at the Université de Sherbrooke.
The culture shock was gentler this time: it wasn't my first, and my family was right there to help me land. I met people who made me grow in ways no diploma could.

chapters.title: What keeps me whole
body: Away from the screen, you'll find me at the gym, at the piano composing, or chasing new flavours in the kitchen — when I'm motivated, I get very hands-on.
I'm a hard worker in my own way, and I love to travel. The world has far too much to offer to turn my back on it.

chapters.title: Where I'm headed
body: Today I build reliable data systems, and I'm exploring how AI can make them genuinely useful to people — not just to machines.
I want to keep bridging the technical and the human: rigorous with data, warm with people, and always learning.

closing.title: To be continued
closing.body: There's one more page waiting to be written.
closing.testimonialSoon: A short video message from me — coming soon.
